content_en:
3. Love of the worldly life

Satan adorns this worldly life and makes it seem good in the hearts of many people, so they have willingly embraced it and held tight to it; they compete with envy, and fight each other for it; it is all the planning of Iblis who said: (My Lord, because You misled me, I will make things on the earth seem good to them, and I will mislead them all)’ What a pity, they have indeed followed him and obeyed him, except for the ones who sought refuge in Allah, and rejected the temptations of this worldly life: (Iblis was correct in his assessment of them and they followed him, except for a group of the believers)‘.

If people had perceived the reality of this worldly life, they would not have paid too much attention to it, or place it in their hearts; indeed Allah 3% has clearly shown its nature: (Know that the life of this world is merely a game and a diversion and ostentation, and a

‘ Recorded by al-Bukhari.

? Recorded by al-Bukhari and Muslim. > Surat al-Hijr, verse 39.

4 Surat Saba’, verse 20.

cause of boasting among yoursélves, and trying to undo one another in wealth and children: like the plant-growth after rain which delights the cultivators, but then it withers and you see it turning yellow, and then it becomes broken stubble. In the Hereafter, there is terrible punishment but also forgiveness from Allah, and His good pleasure. The life of this world is nothing but the enjoyment of delusion)'.

Therefore, this worldly life is merely a game, a diversion and ostentation; and the conscious man is the one who allows it to become a plantation for the Hereafter. It is for this reason that Allah 3 addressed us, saying: (Race each other for forgiveness from your Lord and to a Garden, whose breadth is like that of heaven and earth combined, made ready for those who believe in Allah and His Messengers)”, Likewise, the Prophet #% warned us against being tempted by this worldly life, when he said: “The world is sweet and green (alluring), and verily Allah is going to install you as vicegerent in it in order to see how you act; so avoid the allurement of women.”?

‘Abdullah bin ‘Amru 4 reported that the Prophet 38 said: “He who is successful has accepted Islam, been provided with sufficient for his needs, and made contented, by Allah, with what He has given.”

‘Abdullah ibn al-Shikbir 4 said: “I came to the Prophet # as he was reciting (Abundance diverts you); so he # said: ‘The son of Adam claims: ‘My wealth, my wealth,’ and he added: ‘O son of Adam, is there anything that you claim as your belongings as that which you consumed, utilised, or wore until it was worn out, or you gave away in charity and sent it forward?”

' Surat al-Hadid, verse 20.

? Surat al-Hadid, verse 21.

3 Recorded by Muslim, on the authority of Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri 2. * Recorded by Muslim, al-Tirmidhi and Ibn Majh.

> Recorded by al-Bukhari, Muslim and al-Tirmidhi.

Jabir ibn ‘Abdillah .% said: “The Messenger of Allah 4 happened to be walking through the market place with people on both sides of him. There he found a dead lamb with very short ears; he took hold of its ear, saying: ‘Who amongst you wants to have this for a dirham?’ They replied: ‘We do not even want to have it for less than that, as it is of no use to us.’ He 4% asked: ‘Do you wish to have it free of charge?’ They replied: ‘By Allah, even if it were alive, we would not like to have it; for there is a defect in it, as its ears are very short, and now it is dead also.’ Thereupon, the Prophet 4% said: ‘By Allah, this world is more insignificant in the Eyes of Allah as this dead lamb is in your eyes.””!

Abu Hurayrah said: “I heard the Messenger of Allah saying: ‘This world is cursed, as are all the things in it, except the remembrance of Allah and that which He likes, and scholars and students.””*

‘Amru ibn ‘Awf al-Ansari 4 said: “The Messenger of Allah 4 said: ‘By Allah, I am not afraid for your poverty, but I am afraid that you will lead a life of luxury, as past nations have done; whereupon you will compete with each other for it, as they competed for it, and it will destroy you as it destroyed them.””?

The love of this world has exceeded its limits in the hearts of some people, such that they worship it instead of Allah 8. Abu Hurayrah # reported that the Messenger of Allah 4% said: “Let the slave of dinar, dirham, and khamisa (luxury clothes) perish; for he is (only) pleased if he is given these things, yet displeased if (he is) not.”

If they had know its value, with regard to the Hereafter, they would have rejected it, and sought the Hereafter instead. The Messenger of Allah 4% said: “This world is as insignificant, in comparison to the Hereafter, as if one of you should dip his finger in the ocean and then he should see what has stuck to it.”*

Sahl ibn Sa‘d ¢ said: “I heard the Prophet 4 saying: ‘To guard the Muslims from the infidels, in Allah’s cause, for one day is better than

' Recorded by Muslim.

2 Recorded by al-Tirmidhi.

3 Recorded by al-Bukhari and Muslim. 4 Recorded by al-Bukhari.

5 Recorded by Muslim.

the world and whatever is on its surface, and a place as small as that occupied by the whip, of one of you, is better than the world and whatever is on its surface, and a morning’s or an evening’s journey which a slave of Allah travels in His cause, is better than the world and whatever is on its surface.”

‘Ali ibn Abi Talib 2 said: “He who combines the following features, has indeed sought Paradise and kept himself away from Hell: he knew Allah and obeyed Him; he knew Satan and disobeyed him; he knew the truth and followed it; he knew falsehood and steered clear of it; he knew the (temptations of the) worldly life and rejected them; and he knew the Hereafter and asked for it”.

You should know that if your love for this world overcomes the heart, Satan opens another door:

If a person’s hope lasts long, he/she becomes reluctant towards his/her religious duties, by indulging in worldly matters and destroying his/her potential for the Hereafter. Abu Hurayrah 4 said: “I heard the Prophet # say: ‘The heart of an old man remains young in two respects: his love for the world (its wealth, amusements and luxuries) and his incessant hope.”?

Al-Bukhari said: “‘Ali ibn Abi Talib said: ‘This world is lasting, and the Hereafter is drawing near, and each one of them has its people; so be of the people of the Hereafter, and not among the people of the world, because today is a day of work without reckoning, but tomorrow is the Day of Reckoning and no further work.”

‘Abdullah ibn ‘Umar 2 said: “The Messenger of Allah took hold of my shoulder and said: ‘Be in this world as if you were a stranger or a traveller”. Ibn ‘Umar said: “If you survive till the evening, do not

expect to be alive in the morning, and if you survive till the morning,

' Recorded by al-Bukhari ? Recorded by al-Bukhari and Muslim. > Fath al-bari (11/235).

do not expect to be alive in the evening, and take from your health for your sickness, and (take) from your life for your death.”

The Prophet # showed his Companions the short life of man and his lasting hope, with the help of a small diagram. ‘Abdullah ibn Mas‘ud & said: “The Messenger of Allah drew a square and then drew a line through the middle of it, letting it extend outside the square, and then he drew some short lines attached to that central line, saying: ‘This is a human being, and this, (the square) is his lease of life, surrounding him from all sides, and this line which is outside the square is his hope, and these short lines are the calamities and troubles which may befall him, and if one misses him, another will hit (overcome) him, and if the other misses him, a third one will hit him.”

So my dear Muslim brother/sister, avoid having this long lasting hope, because it breeds ineffective work, and it opens another door for Satan.

Ka‘b ibn ‘Ayad # said: “I heard the Messenger of Allah # say: “Every nation has a test to undergo, my nation will be tried through wealth.’”*

Al-Qurtubi said: “The meaning of this Hadith is that beneficial wealth and being praiseworthy is to be found in self-contentment,” (i.e. if the person feels self-content, he renounces greediness, and he obtains

' Recorded by Mujahid.

* Recorded by al-Bukhari.

> Recorded by al-Tirmidhi.

4 Recorded by al-Tirmidhi,

* Recorded by al-Bukhari and Muslim.

more dignity and praise than the one whose self is weak because of his cupidity, which involves him in vice, to become despicable and despised by all people. The man with self-contentment accepts his designated provision from Allah 3#; he is not keen to get more, if not in need. It is a total submission to the Divine Decree of Allah 3% and. His Command, and the strong belief that what Allah 4% reserves for His believing Servants is better and ever-lasting.)

So avoid being greedy because it takes away one’s religion and dignity, and allows Satan to open the door for another similar bad characteristic:

Allah 4 said: (Satan promises you poverty and commands you to avarice. Allah promises you Forgiveness from Himself and Abundance; Allah is All-Encompassing, All-Knowing)'. Satan threatens man with poverty in order that he does not spend for the Sake of Allah; he makes him believe that if he does spend in the Cause of Allah, he would be left in poverty or need. But Allah 4 assures every sincere believer: (Allah promises you Forgiveness from Himself and Abundance; Allah is All-Encompassing, All-Knowing) (i.e. the wealth of Allah is unlimited and His Provision for his servants is unending and infinite.)
content_id:
3. Cinta terhadap kehidupan dunia

Setan menghiasi kehidupan dunia ini dan menjadikannya tampak indah di hati banyak orang, sehingga mereka dengan sukarela memeluknya dan berpegang teguh padanya; mereka saling berlomba dengan iri hati, dan saling berperang memperebutkannya; semua itu adalah rencana Iblis yang berkata: (Ya Tuhanku, karena Engkau telah menyesatkanku, aku akan menjadikan segala sesuatu di bumi tampak indah bagi mereka, dan aku akan menyesatkan mereka semua)' Sungguh disayangkan, mereka memang telah mengikuti dan menaatinya, kecuali orang-orang yang berlindung kepada Allah, dan menolak godaan kehidupan dunia ini: (Iblis benar dalam penilaiannya terhadap mereka dan mereka mengikutinya, kecuali sekelompok orang-orang beriman)'.

Seandainya manusia menyadari hakikat kehidupan dunia ini, mereka tidak akan terlalu memperhatikannya, atau menempatkannya di dalam hati mereka; sesungguhnya Allah 3% telah menjelaskan dengan gamblang sifatnya: (Ketahuilah bahwa kehidupan dunia ini hanyalah permainan dan senda gurau dan perhiasan, dan

' Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari.

? Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim. > Surat al-Hijr, ayat 39.

4 Surat Saba', ayat 20.

sebab saling bermegah-megahan di antara kamu, serta berlomba-lomba dalam kekayaan dan anak: seperti tanaman setelah hujan yang mengagumkan para petani, tetapi kemudian ia mengering dan kamu melihatnya menguning, lalu ia menjadi reruntuhan kering. Di akhirat, ada azab yang pedih tetapi juga ampunan dari Allah, dan keridhaan-Nya. Kehidupan dunia ini tidak lain hanyalah kesenangan yang memperdaya)'.

Oleh karena itu, kehidupan dunia ini hanyalah permainan, senda gurau dan perhiasan; dan orang yang sadar adalah orang yang menjadikannya sebagai ladang untuk akhirat. Karena alasan inilah Allah 3 menyeru kita, dengan berfirman: (Berlomba-lombalah kamu untuk mendapat ampunan dari Tuhanmu dan surga yang luasnya seluas langit dan bumi, yang disediakan bagi orang-orang yang beriman kepada Allah dan para Rasul-Nya)", Demikian pula, Nabi #% memperingatkan kita agar tidak tergoda oleh kehidupan dunia ini, ketika beliau bersabda: "Dunia ini manis dan hijau (memikat), dan sesungguhnya Allah akan menjadikan kamu sebagai khalifah di dalamnya untuk melihat bagaimana kamu berbuat; maka hindarilah godaan wanita."?

'Abdullah bin 'Amru 4 meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi 38 bersabda: "Orang yang beruntung adalah yang telah memeluk Islam, diberi kecukupan dalam kebutuhannya, dan dijadikan merasa puas oleh Allah dengan apa yang telah Dia berikan kepadanya."

'Abdullah ibn al-Shikbir 4 berkata: "Aku datang kepada Nabi # ketika beliau sedang membaca (Bermegah-megahan telah melalaikan kamu); lalu beliau # bersabda: 'Anak Adam mengaku: 'Hartaku, hartaku,' dan beliau menambahkan: 'Wahai anak Adam, adakah sesuatu yang kamu klaim sebagai milikmu selain yang kamu makan lalu habis, yang kamu pakai lalu usang, atau yang kamu sedekahkan dan kamu kirimkan ke depan?'"

' Surat al-Hadid, ayat 20.

? Surat al-Hadid, ayat 21.

3 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim, dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri 2. * Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim, al-Tirmidhi dan Ibn Majah.

> Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari, Muslim dan al-Tirmidhi.

Jabir ibn 'Abdillah .% berkata: "Rasulullah 4 kebetulan sedang berjalan melewati pasar dengan orang-orang di kedua sisinya. Di sana beliau menemukan seekor kambing mati dengan telinga yang sangat pendek; beliau memegang telinganya, seraya berkata: 'Siapa di antara kalian yang ingin memiliki ini seharga satu dirham?' Mereka menjawab: 'Kami bahkan tidak menginginkannya dengan harga kurang dari itu, karena tidak berguna bagi kami.' Beliau 4% bertanya: 'Apakah kalian menginginkannya secara gratis?' Mereka menjawab: 'Demi Allah, bahkan jika ia masih hidup, kami tidak mau memilikinya; karena ada cacat padanya, telinganya sangat pendek, dan sekarang ia sudah mati pula.' Maka Nabi 4% bersabda: 'Demi Allah, dunia ini lebih hina di Sisi Allah daripada kambing mati ini di mata kalian.'"!

Abu Hurayrah berkata: "Aku mendengar Rasulullah bersabda: 'Dunia ini terlaknat, begitu pula segala sesuatu yang ada di dalamnya, kecuali dzikir kepada Allah dan apa yang Dia sukai, serta para ulama dan para penuntut ilmu.'"*

'Amru ibn 'Awf al-Ansari 4 berkata: "Rasulullah 4 bersabda: 'Demi Allah, bukan kemiskinan kalian yang aku khawatirkan, tetapi aku khawatir kalian akan menjalani kehidupan mewah, sebagaimana umat-umat terdahulu; lalu kalian akan saling berlomba memperebutkannya, sebagaimana mereka berlomba, dan ia akan menghancurkan kalian sebagaimana ia menghancurkan mereka.'"?

Cinta dunia telah melampaui batasnya di hati sebagian orang, sehingga mereka menyembahnya selain Allah 8. Abu Hurayrah # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah 4% bersabda: "Celakalah hamba dinar, dirham, dan khamisa (pakaian mewah); karena dia hanya senang jika diberi hal-hal tersebut, namun tidak puas jika tidak diberi."

Seandainya mereka mengetahui nilainya, dibandingkan dengan akhirat, mereka pasti akan menolaknya, dan mencari akhirat sebagai gantinya. Rasulullah 4% bersabda: "Dunia ini sama tidak berartinya, dibandingkan dengan akhirat, seperti jika salah seorang dari kalian mencelupkan jarinya ke lautan lalu melihat apa yang melekat padanya."*

Sahl ibn Sa'd ¢ berkata: "Aku mendengar Nabi 4 bersabda: 'Berjaga menjaga kaum muslimin dari orang-orang kafir, di jalan Allah, selama satu hari lebih baik daripada

' Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim.

2 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Tirmidhi.

3 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim. 4 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari.

5 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim.

dunia dan segala isinya, dan suatu tempat seluas cambuk salah seorang dari kalian, lebih baik daripada dunia dan segala isinya, dan perjalanan pagi atau sore yang ditempuh seorang hamba Allah di jalan-Nya, lebih baik daripada dunia dan segala isinya."

'Ali ibn Abi Talib 2 berkata: "Barangsiapa menghimpun sifat-sifat berikut, sungguh ia telah mengejar surga dan menjauhkan dirinya dari neraka: ia mengenal Allah dan menaati-Nya; ia mengenal setan dan mendurhakai-nya; ia mengenal kebenaran dan mengikutinya; ia mengenal kebatilan dan menjauhinya; ia mengenal godaan kehidupan dunia dan menolaknya; dan ia mengenal akhirat dan memohonnya."

Ketahuilah bahwa jika cintamu terhadap dunia ini menguasai hati, setan akan membuka pintu yang lain:

Jika harapan seseorang berlangsung lama, ia menjadi enggan terhadap kewajiban agamanya, dengan tenggelam dalam urusan duniawi dan menghancurkan potensinya untuk akhirat. Abu Hurayrah 4 berkata: "Aku mendengar Nabi # bersabda: 'Hati orang tua tetap muda dalam dua hal: cintanya terhadap dunia (kekayaan, hiburan dan kemewahan) dan harapannya yang tiada henti.'"?

Al-Bukhari berkata: "'Ali ibn Abi Talib berkata: 'Dunia ini sedang berlalu, dan akhirat sedang mendekat, dan masing-masing memiliki penghuninya; maka jadilah penghuni akhirat, dan jangan menjadi penghuni dunia, karena hari ini adalah hari beramal tanpa perhitungan, tetapi besok adalah Hari Perhitungan dan tidak ada lagi amal.'"

'Abdullah ibn 'Umar 2 berkata: "Rasulullah memegang bahuku dan bersabda: 'Jadilah di dunia ini seolah-olah kamu orang asing atau musafir'". Ibn 'Umar berkata: "Jika kamu bertahan hingga sore, jangan

berharap masih hidup di pagi hari, dan jika kamu bertahan hingga pagi,

' Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari ? Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim. > Fath al-bari (11/235).

jangan berharap masih hidup di sore hari, dan ambillah dari kesehatanmu untuk sakitmu, dan (ambillah) dari hidupmu untuk kematianmu."

Nabi # menunjukkan kepada para Sahabatnya pendeknya umur manusia dan panjangnya harapannya, dengan bantuan sebuah diagram kecil. 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud & berkata: "Rasulullah menggambar sebuah persegi lalu menggambar sebuah garis melewati tengahnya, membiarkannya memanjang keluar dari persegi tersebut, kemudian beliau menggambar beberapa garis pendek yang melekat pada garis tengah itu, seraya bersabda: 'Ini adalah manusia, dan ini, (persegi tersebut) adalah batas umurnya, yang mengitarinya dari segala sisi, dan garis yang berada di luar persegi ini adalah harapannya, dan garis-garis pendek ini adalah musibah-musibah dan kesulitan-kesulitan yang mungkin menimpanya, dan jika yang satu meleset darinya, yang lain akan mengenainya, dan jika yang lain meleset darinya, yang ketiga akan mengenainya.'"

Maka wahai saudaraku sesama Muslim, hindarilah harapan yang berkepanjangan ini, karena ia melahirkan amal yang sia-sia, dan ia membuka pintu lain bagi setan.

Ka'b ibn 'Ayad # berkata: "Aku mendengar Rasulullah # bersabda: 'Setiap umat memiliki ujian yang harus dilaluinya, ujian umatku adalah melalui harta.'"*

Al-Qurtubi berkata: "Makna hadith ini adalah bahwa harta yang bermanfaat dan terpuji ditemukan dalam sikap qana'ah (merasa cukup)," (yaitu jika seseorang merasa cukup, ia meninggalkan keserakahan, dan ia memperoleh

' Diriwayatkan oleh Mujahid.

* Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari.

> Diriwayatkan oleh al-Tirmidhi.

4 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Tirmidhi,

* Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim.

lebih bermartabat dan terpuji daripada orang yang jiwanya lemah karena ketamakannya, yang melibatkannya dalam keburukan, sehingga menjadi hina dan direndahkan oleh semua orang. Orang yang memiliki qana'ah menerima rezeki yang telah Allah 3# tetapkan untuknya; ia tidak berambisi untuk mendapatkan lebih, jika tidak membutuhkannya. Ini adalah kepasrahan total terhadap Takdir Allah 3% dan Perintah-Nya, serta keyakinan yang kuat bahwa apa yang Allah 4% simpan bagi hamba-hamba-Nya yang beriman adalah lebih baik dan kekal.)

Maka jauhilah sifat tamak karena ia menghilangkan agama dan kehormatan seseorang, serta memungkinkan Setan membuka pintu bagi sifat buruk lainnya yang serupa:

Allah 4 berfirman: (Setan menjanjikan kamu kemiskinan dan memerintahkan kamu berbuat kekikiran. Allah menjanjikan kamu ampunan dari-Nya dan karunia; Allah Maha Luas, Maha Mengetahui)'. Setan mengancam manusia dengan kemiskinan agar ia tidak berinfak di jalan Allah; ia membuat manusia percaya bahwa jika ia berinfak di jalan Allah, ia akan ditinggalkan dalam kemiskinan atau kekurangan. Namun Allah 4 meyakinkan setiap mukmin yang ikhlas: (Allah menjanjikan kamu ampunan dari-Nya dan karunia; Allah Maha Luas, Maha Mengetahui) (yaitu kekayaan Allah tidak terbatas dan rezeki-Nya bagi hamba-hamba-Nya tidak pernah habis dan tidak terhingga.)
content_ar: 3. المحبة of the worldly الحياة الشيطان adorns this worldly الحياة and makes it seem الخير in the القلوب of many people, لذا they have willingly embraced it and held tight to it; they compete with الحسد, and fight بعضهم بعضاً for it; it is all the planning of إبليس who said: (My Lord, لأن You misled me, I will make things on the earth seem الخير to them, and I will mislead them all)’ What a pity, they have indeed followed him and obeyed him, except for the ones who sought refuge in الله, and rejected the temptations of this worldly الحياة: (إبليس was correct in his assessment of them and they followed him, except for a group of the المؤمنون)‘. إذا people had perceived the reality of this worldly الحياة, they would not have paid أيضاً much attention to it, or place it in their القلوب; indeed الله 3% has clearly shown its nature: (Know that the الحياة of this الدنيا is merely a game and a diversion and ostentation, and a ‘ Recorded by البخاري. ? Recorded by البخاري and مسلم. > Surat al-Hijr, الآية 39. 4 Surat Saba’, الآية 20. cause of boasting among yoursélves, and trying to undo بعضهم بعضاً in الثروة and الأولاد: like the plant-growth بعد rain which delights the cultivators, but then it withers and you see it turning yellow, and then it becomes broken stubble. In الآخرة, there is terrible العقاب but أيضاً المغفرة from الله, and His الخير pleasure. The الحياة of this الدنيا is nothing but the enjoyment of delusion)'. لذلك, this worldly الحياة is merely a game, a diversion and ostentation; and the conscious man is the one who allows it to become a plantation for الآخرة. It is for this العقل that الله 3 addressed us, saying: (Race بعضهم بعضاً for المغفرة from your Lord and to a Garden, whose breadth is like that of heaven and earth combined, made ready for those who believe in الله and His الرسل)”, Likewise, النبي ﷺ #% warned us against being tempted by this worldly الحياة, عندما قال: “The الدنيا is sweet and green (alluring), and verily الله is going to install you as vicegerent in it من أجل see how you act; لذا avoid the allurement of women.”? ‘Abdullah bin ‘Amru 4 reported that النبي ﷺ 38 said: “He who is successful has accepted الإسلام, been provided with sufficient for his needs, and made contented, by الله, with what He has given.” ‘Abdullah ibn al-Shikbir 4 said: “I came to النبي ﷺ # as he was reciting (Abundance diverts you); لذا he # said: ‘The son of آدم claims: ‘My الثروة, my الثروة,’ and he added: ‘O son of آدم, is there anything that you claim as your belongings as that which you consumed, utilised, or wore حتى it was worn out, or you gave away in الصدقة and sent it forward?” ' Surat al-Hadid, الآية 20. ? Surat al-Hadid, الآية 21. 3 Recorded by مسلم, on the authority of Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri 2. * Recorded by مسلم, al-الترمذي and Ibn Majh. > Recorded by البخاري, مسلم and al-الترمذي.</p
